steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The Art Institute houses 300,000 works. Visitors looking to ______ their art-viewing experience "should turn to Chicago’s streets," where additional works can be found. The blank must mean to add to or extend an existing experience.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'mitigate': means to lessen or reduce. Turning to more art would not reduce the experience. ✗
  • Choice B — 'satisfy': means to fulfill. This implies the experience is already complete, which contradicts the idea of seeking more art. ✗
  • Choice C — 'provoke': means to stimulate or irritate. The passage is not about triggering a reaction; it’s about adding to what the museum offers. ✗
  • Choice D — 'supplement': means to add to something to make it more complete. Substituting: "visitors looking to supplement their art-viewing experience should turn to Chicago’s streets" — this fits perfectly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
